summary: Editor freezing for a long time when editing shader that uses the FastNoiseLite library as gdshaderinc.

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5-dev1.mono, 4.4.1-rc1.mono, 4.4-stable.mono, 4.4-beta3.mono, 4.4-dev7.mono, 4.4-dev4.mono, 4.4-dev3.mono, 4.2.2-stable.mono

### System information

Godot v4.5.dev1.mono - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Fri, 07 Mar 2025 20:19:00 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (RADV NAVI21) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-9900K CPU @ 3.60GHz (16 threads)

### Issue description

I'm working on a text-based sky shader that uses the FastNoiseLite GLSL library through the use of the `#include` directive. I have the viewport in normal rendering mode so that I can see my changes as I work on it. However, whenever I am working on it and I make a change, most of the time the editor will completely freeze for anywhere from ~20 seconds to ~2 minutes. Usually the freeze is over a minute at least. It will max out one of my threads for all of that time while I presume it compiles the shader.

The really confusing part is that if I do the same changes on my laptop that has the following specs:
```
Godot v4.5.dev1.mono - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Sat, 22 Feb 2025 00:37:05 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 (nvidia; 570.86.16) - AMD Ryzen 5 4600H with Radeon Graphics (12 threads)
```
the editor only freezes for ~4 seconds, while still annoying it is way more manageable than having to wait a minute or two on every little change.

As far as I'm aware the i9-9900K on my desktop should outperform the mobile Ryzen 5 4600H in single-core performance? Why is my desktop struggling so much to compile a shader that uses the FastNoiseLite library?

### Steps to reproduce

1. Load the provided MRP.
2. Open `sky_shader.gdshader` in the shader editor.
3. Make some changes one by one to the shader and wait for the editor to freeze for long periods of time as it compiles the shader.
      - Sometimes applying and reverting similar changes doesn't trigger shader compilation.
      - Changes such as adding a new `uniform` variable (The value doesn't need to be used anywhere).
      - Changes such as modifying a number (e.g. changing `UvScale`).
      - Changes such as adding/removing a line of code.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[shaderfreezemrp.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19392981/shaderfreezemrp.zip)

summary: Changing values in an exported dictionary thorugh Remote in the editor breaks object references.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.3 stable and 4.4 stable

### System information

Godot v4.3.stable - Windows 10.0.22631 - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated Quadro T2000 - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-10850H CPU @ 2.70GHz (12 Threads)

### Issue description

When exporting a dictionary that has a key set to refer to an object (like a node), changing any other value in a different key through the editor in "Remote" will break the object reference.

For example, if the dictionary contains a key with a bool value, and a key with an object value, then changing the bool value will change the object to "EncodedObjectAsID" which no longer refer to the original object.

I do not expect an unrelated value to change in a different key when changing the value of another.

Before clicking:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/06bb3344-2ede-45e5-94d2-0899c6dd97c6)

Original object (Marker2D):
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a4cebffd-054b-4cae-b7dc-3f6d22b84a96)

After clicking:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/488a5c70-e466-4a77-9424-a0e7825ccba3)

The broken object:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f568510-a74b-4042-aea3-aaaeb1733abd)

### Steps to reproduce

Create a node with an exported empty dictionary.

In code:
Create a key with any value, can be a bool, string, int.. doesn't really matter.
Create a second key with a reference to a node as a value.

example:
```
@export var dict : Dictionary

func _ready() -> void:
	dict["bool_value"] = false
	dict["object_value"] = $"../Point"
```

Start the game, go to the node with the dictionary in Remote and change the first value. This breaks the object reference.

summary: Physics interpolation affects particle systems

description.text:
### Tested versions

v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

### Issue description

Physics interpolation affects gpu particle systems. This seems wrong, as I don't think particle systems are considered physics objects. It's a problem if you ever want to move particle systems before restarting them in the same frame, as the particles will spawn in between where they're coming from and their destination. 
Workarounds easy, just disable physics interpolation on the particle system.

### Steps to reproduce

Enable physics interpolation in project settings and on a burst particle system and have the system move the same frame restart() is called. The particles shouldn't appear at the correct position, instead somewhere in between the position it traveled from and its destination.

summary: Inconsistent windowed/full screen behavior in 4.x

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.0-4.4 -- this issue has been happening ever since I've been using 4.0.  Didn't happen in 3.x.  I'm guessing it has something to do with changes made to support multiple windows in 4.x.

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6603) - AMD Ryzen 7 3700X 8-Core Processor (16 threads)

### Issue description

On rare occasions, usually when my computer hitches due to other processes taking resources, running my project will cause the window to open in a way that is not what I specified.

For example, I might tell it to be maximized, and the window will start out maximized, but then it seems to open a different window that is not maximized.

Sometimes it will be fullscreen, but not actually considered fullscreen, and the mouse is offset from the UI elements until I enable and disable fullscreen in my game options.

Edit: Finally got a clip of it happening.  Unfortunately I alt-tabbed, so you couldn't see what looks like a second window pop up.  Usually only happens when something is causing my computer to have really slow file access and Godot takes a long time to load stuff.  Not sure why that happens.  Maybe we need to try a delay before and after setting the window mode to reproduce it? https://www.twitch.tv/jitspoe/clip/FunnyRepleteMetalDancingBanana-Qkj4o4aLC0_ek67u

Edit2: Manged to get it to reproduce in the MRP after adding a delay before setting the window mode the first time: https://www.twitch.tv/jitspoe/clip/FurtiveBoldCatRalpherZ-crQAJxDtP8Ol9AyQ (disregard the flying donut)

And again with fullscreen mode: https://www.twitch.tv/jitspoe/clip/CrackyMildAnteaterKevinTurtle-n0WCKi6FokExAQHK

### Steps to reproduce

1. Have a project that starts in windowed mode.
2. Ensure there's a long enough load time to switch windows (this can be done with OS.delay_msec(4000) or so).
3. Have code change the window mode
4. (Might need another load time delay).
5. Launch program with embed game disabled.
6. During delay, click on another window to change focus.
7. Note that window might be in windowed mode instead of maximized or fullscreen or whatever it was set to.

Something like this:
```gdscript
func _ready():
	load_config()

func load_config():
	var config := ConfigFile.new()
	config.load(CONFIG_FILE_NAME)
	var fullscreen = config.get_value(VIDEO_SECTION, "fullscreen", true)
	OS.delay_msec(4000) # Simulate loading time
	get_tree().root.mode = Window.MODE_MAXIMIZED # Necessary to ensure fullscreen doesn't happen on wrong screen.
	if (fullscreen):
		get_tree().root.mode = Window.MODE_EXCLUSIVE_FULLSCREEN
	OS.delay_msec(4000) # Simulate loading time
```

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

I attempted to make a MRP structuring things the same way I had them in my project, but I haven't been able to reproduce it in this or my full project today, so I don't know if it will repro at all on this.  I added a sleep/delay_msec() to try to simulate longer load times but still no luck.
[test_inconsistent_window_mode.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19382215/test_inconsistent_window_mode.zip)


I really just wanted to get this bug entered as it can be a pretty bad user experience when it happens, especially when the UI doesn't line up with the mouse cursor.

The closest I've come to reproducing it is adding a breakpoint right after setting the maximized mode and resizing the window while the breakpoint is there, which results in Godot not being aware it's no longer maximized and stuff being off, but the symptoms are different from the issue that happens when launching my game sometimes.


Edit: This MRP is more consistent at reproducing the issue: 
[test_inconsistent_window_mode2.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19383364/test_inconsistent_window_mode2.zip)

summary: Nested HSPlitContainers acting weird

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot Engine v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - AMD Radeon RX 6800 XT (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 9 3950X 16-Core Processor (32 threads)

### Issue description

While I'm experimenting with different layout options using godot UI components i stumbled accross this weird behavior that i would categorize as a bug.

When dragging the Slider of the HSplitContainer to the right until my RightPanel is pushed out of the view, contents of the second half of the HSplitContainer start to shift to the left i.e. "bleed out".

Although the documentation says HSplitContainer supports only two child elements - in this case i nested the second half with another HSplitContainer and VSplitContainer - i would expect that the setup should still work and at least not push ColorRects out of their containers nor should the slider draw behind the ColorRects.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2621b00c-d037-445f-bc98-f1709f8f3e95)

To get an idea what it looks like when starting the project:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c92f488-5bfb-439f-99a0-fe821be734dc)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create the following Layout using view 1920x1080
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9f480b7c-deae-42be-93d0-25f512dc1a7d)
2. Attach a script to Layout Node using BaseClass Control
3. Programatically instance via ColorRect.new(), set a color and add them as child to
```
$StatusBar.add_child(generate_rect("#4F5F79"))
$ContentWrapper/HSplitContainer/LeftPanel/VSplitContainer.add_child(generate_rect("#0052CC"))
$ContentWrapper/HSplitContainer/ContentPanel/HSplitContainer/MiddleContainer/TopMiddle.add_child(generate_rect("#00B7D8"))
$ContentWrapper/HSplitContainer/ContentPanel/HSplitContainer/MiddleContainer/BottomMiddle.add_child(generate_rect("#6554C1"))
$ContentWrapper/HSplitContainer/ContentPanel/HSplitContainer/RightContainer.add_child("#FF8B00"))
```
I would think you can also add them via Editor. Shouldnt make a difference.

I used the following function to accomplish this
```
func generate_rect(color_code: String) -> ColorRect:
  var new_rect = ColorRect.new()
  new_rect.color = Color.from_string(color_code)
  new_rect.size_flags_horizontal = Control.SIZE_EXPAND_FILL
  new_rect.size_flags_vertical = Control.SIZE_EXPAND_FILL
  return new_rect
```
4. Set custom minimum sizes Top-Down
```
Layout: Anchors, Full Rect
NavBar: Anchors, Top Wide - Custom Minimum Size: 0px 40 px
Panel ContentWrapper: Anchros, Custom: 0,0,1.0,1.0 0px 40px 0px -30px
HSplitContainer: Anchros, Full Rect
LeftPanel: Fill H and V, Custom Minimum Size: 450px 0px
ContentPanel: Fill H and V, Set to expand
HSplitContainer: Anchors, Full Rect
MiddleContainer (VSplitContainer): Fill H and V, Set to expand - Custom Minimum Size: 1000px 0px
TopMiddle (HSplitContainer): Custom Minimum Size: 0px 650px
BottomMiddle (HSplitContainer): Sizes automatically according to this setup
RightContainer: Fill H and V, Set to expand
StatusBar: Anchors, Bottom Wide, Custom Minimum Size: 0px 30px
```
5. Run the project and move the slider to the right until the RightContainer starts to get moved out of the view.
6. Observe Dragger beeing under child elements and see that ColorRects start to stay in place according to their Custom Minium Size.

You could also shrink the RightPanel so its no longer visible and then start Dragging with the left slider to reproduce this a bit quicker.

**Demo**

[GDUIGlitch-v1.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19377064/GDUIGlitch-v1.zip)

summary: iOS setting "Hide Home Indicator" breaks "Suppress UI Gesture", they should not both be default

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee], v4.3.stable.official [77dcf97d8].

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.2.0) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M3 Max - Apple M3 Max (16 threads)

### Issue description

With default settings, the iOS-specific project setting `display/window/ios/suppress_ui_gesture` ("Suppress UI Gesture") does not work as intended.

This option, when enabled, is intended according to its documentation to require two swipes to activate iOS system gestures, instead of the normal one. This is important for immersive apps like games that don't want accidental gestures to trigger iOS UI features.

On newer versions of iOS, this does NOT work. Even with the setting enabled, a single swipe will minimize the app.

After some investigation, I found that disabling the separate iOS project setting `display/window/ios/hide_home_indicator` ("Hide Home Indicator") causes "Suppress UI Gesture" to work correctly.

Here is a video somewhat demonstrating the problem and workaround with "Hide Home Indicator" disabled: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_xSAs7EZfQ8 (sorry, the video from the simulator doesn't show the touch, but I think it's somewhat clear what's going on)

Why does disabling "Hide Home Indicator" work? The Apple documentation on the features ([Suppress UI Gesture](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiviewcontroller/preferredscreenedgesdeferringsystemgestures?language=objc), [Hide Home Indicator](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uiviewcontroller/prefershomeindicatorautohidden?language=objc)) doesn't mention any incompatibility.

However, there are a couple of StackOverflow threads where users are reporting that these settings are incompatible, and only one or the other can be used:

- https://stackoverflow.com/a/47806461 ("It is a choice between hidden and deferred but NOT both")

- https://stackoverflow.com/a/52875222 ("Removing prefersHomeIndicatorAutoHidden makes it work")

This matches [my experience](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_xSAs7EZfQ8) so I believe this conclusion.

The action item for Godot is to decide what to do about this. My suggestions:

1. Disable "Hide Home Indicator" by default.

2. Document both of these settings to mention their incompatibility.

3. If they're both checked, there should be some kind of warning, somewhere. Ideally in the settings UI.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create an empty project and add an iOS export.

2. Run on a simulator or real device on a recent version of iOS. I'm using iPhone 16 Pro on iOS 18.2.

3. Swipe up from the bottom of the screen to minimize the app.

4. With default settings, there is no "home" bar visible (this is because `display/window/ios/hide_home_indicator` ("Hide Home Indicator") is enabled by default), and it takes ONE swipe to minimize the app. This is NOT intended, because by default `display/window/ios/suppress_ui_gesture` ("Suppress UI Gesture") is enabled, which is intended to require TWO swipes to be able to minimize.

5. Disable "Hide Home Indicator" and run the app again on a simulator or real device on a recent version of iOS.

6. Observe that the "home" bar is now visible (this is because `display/window/ios/hide_home_indicator`, "Hide Home Indicator" is disabled).

6. Swipe up from the bottom and observe that it now requires TWO swipes to minimize, as intended according to the enabled-by-default "Suppress UI Gesture" setting).

summary: Fix connections dialog connecting to signal before adding new script function

description.text:
Fixes part of (?) #85528, specifically https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/85528#issuecomment-2355347392 (I've tested only with GDScript though).

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/85528#issuecomment-1836988143 pinpointed the issue well, a Callable with binds failed to be connected because of a different check when connecting:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ef1153baf3343470b65258421a163c92455d32b3/core/object/object.cpp#L1421-L1428

Specifically `p_callable.is_valid()` fails because `get_custom()->is_valid()`  fails, which fails because `custom->get_object()->has_method(get_method())` fails:
https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/ef1153baf3343470b65258421a163c92455d32b3/core/variant/callable.cpp#L152-L158

But why does the object has no method which we've just told the dialog to create? Turns out the connections dialog firstly tries to connect the method, then it adds the method. This PR swaps the order of these. No idea if/why connecting would need to happen before adding the method. :thinking:

summary: GLTF animation imports unused tracks for all animations

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4 .Net

### System information

Win10 x64 22H2

### Issue description

Importing .Blend directly or GLTF/GLB that has animations will add unused tracks for all animated objects in each animation.

When playing these animations, each one overrides the other because each animation is trying to control all the animated objects, even though most of the objects don't belong to that animation.

For example, let's say .Blend/GLTF/GLB file has Cube1 and Cube2. 
Cube1 has two keyframes that animate Cube1 properties only.
Cube2 has two keyframes that animate Cube2 properties only.

When importing into Godot:
The animation for Cube1 will contain Cube1 keyframes AND a Cube2 keyframe (at rest).
The animation for Cube2 will contain Cube2 keyframes AND a Cube1 keyframe (at rest).

When playing Cube1 and Cube2 animations at the same time, they try to override each other and most animated objects don't animate properly. This is because Cube1 animation is trying to animate Cube1 and Cube2, even though it should only animate Cube1. Vice versa with Cube2.

I confirm this is a Godot import issue because opening the .GLTF/.GLB file into Blender shows each animation only contains keyframes for it's animated object.

Using a different GLTF viewer like this animates properly.
https://gltf-viewer.donmccurdy.com/

This is a big issue because the only way to fix this is to go through each animation in Godot and manually delete all the unused keyframes.

A Reddit thread mentions this issue as well:
https://old.reddit.com/r/godot/comments/17zeisa/exclude_animation_tracks_for_bones_not_in_action/

### Steps to reproduce

1) Open .Blend file and confirm Cube1 and Cube2 each have keyframes that belong only to itself.
2) Import .Blend or GLTF version into Godot.
3) Open the animations in Godot and see that Cube1 animations include Cube2 keyframes, and vice versa with Cube2.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09c840be-ea7f-4a07-a43d-ba43cfc66b72)

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Here's the .Blend file.

summary: `@export_file` need to allow you to keep the old behavior of setting it as res path instead of uid if desired (breaking change!)

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v4.4.stable.flathub [4c311cbee]
- Not reproducible in v4.3.stable.flathub
- Introduced by #97912

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable (4c311cbee) - Freedesktop SDK 24.08 (Flatpak runtime) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) HD Graphics 5500 (BDW GT2) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-5300U CPU @ 2.30GHz (4 threads)

### Issue description

The change to how `@export_file` works is a breaking change!
I was relying on the res path structure (changing .ogg extension of an audio file to an external .mp3 on web platform, which I'm playing via JS to avoid the audio issues on single thread and to stream it out of the pck).

This is bad because there's no indication that the actual path is an uid now unless you inspect the tscn -- the inspector still shows it as a res path, and since it's a string type, of course you'd expect it to retain its previous behavior consistent with `@export_dir`.
I can understand the reasoning for it is to update on refactoring, but I wish this was handled differently (e.g. keep track of it somehow, e.g. by adding a uid "metadata" instead of replacing it with a uid).

Since that's how it is now, please at least do these:

#### 1. Document it properly
Docs for `@export_file` still say it's a "path to a file". An uid is not what one would expect from this wording.
More importantly, please add this as a breaking change in [Upgrading from Godot 4.3 to Godot 4.4](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/migrating/upgrading_to_godot_4.4.html) docs page.

#### 2. Please let us decide if we want an uid or path
There's valid use cases for preferring a res path too!
If possible, I suggest adding a parameter on `@export_file` to keep the old behavior if desired. E.g:
```gdscript
@export_file(as_uid: bool, filter: String = "", ...) vararg # if false, export by path; if true export by uid
@export_file(filter: String = "", ...) vararg # use a default from project settings
```

#### 3. Add a project setting so we can choose the default mode
This would apply when `@export_file` doesn't specify the parameter, so we can keep the old behavior when upgrading to Godot 4.4.
You can make it by res path by default (for upgrading projects) but set it to uid on new projects.

#### 4. Please add clarity in the inspector
Add some UI in the inspector so we can know if its a uid or res path.
E.g. a checkbox so we can toggle between uid or res path.
Or if it's not toggleable directly, an icon that shows when it's actually an uid.
Or maybe show it as the uid and show the inferred res path on hover in a tooltip or below the field.

### Steps to reproduce

Just make a script with `@export_file` and set a project file in the inspector.
Look at the tscn and notice it's actually a uid, even though the inspector say it's a res path.
If you were relying on the previous behavior, your project will break on 4.4.

Example use cases that break:
- Changing extension of a file to play a different external audio on web, that you need out of the pck.
- Get resources dynamically relying on path structure e.g. in a custom localization solution where you may want to add/change a locale folder dynamically in the res path.
- Any behavior that changes dynamically based on an expected consistent path structure.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Fix issue of `AnimationPlayer` hiding bezier editor when re-selecting it

description.text:
Fixes #102654.

This fixes the issue of the Animation pane always switching away from the bezier view when clicking an `AnimationPlayer` instance, or when selecting another animation in the same `AnimationPlayer` instance.

This is achieved by swapping out the current use of `_cancel_bezier_edit()` in `AnimationTrackEditor::set_animation` with `_check_bezier_exist()` instead, which only closes the bezier view if there are no bezier tracks in the new animation, and then we call `_bezier_edit` on the first bezier track we find in the potentially new animation, to update the interface.

The `_check_bezier_exist()` method had to be slightly modified to be able to deal with an invalid `animation` reference as well.

Here's what it looked like before:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/35a0f996-2761-48d8-aecf-fc1d078227e8

Here's what it looks like with this PR:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2746b6ab-bde8-4480-a84f-1b87e5731e8d

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Godot 4.4 stable and 4.4-rc1

### System information

Godot v4.4.1.rc1 - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - integrated AMD Radeon 780M Graphics (Advanced Micro Devices, Inc.; 32.0.12033.1030) - AMD Ryzen 7 7840HS w/ Radeon 780M Graphics (16 threads)

### Issue description

The project icon in the Project Manager fails to update when the icon file contains non-ASCII characters (e.g., Chinese characters) in its filename. This issue affects both PNG and SVG formats. The icon remains as default regardless of the file being properly set in project settings.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3f91959c-e1ce-4c69-8913-411352acb72c)

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/55261dee-d4df-48ae-b1f1-25108cce7ef8)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open Project Manager
2. Create a new project or open existing project
3. Go to Project Settings
4. Navigate to Application > Config > Icon
5. Set a custom icon file with non-ASCII characters in filename (e.g., "项目图标.png" or "图标.svg")
6. Save settings and restart the editor
7. Check Project Manager
8. (Verification) Rename the same icon file to ASCII-only characters (e.g., "icon.png") - this works correctly

**Expected behavior:**
The project should display the custom icon regardless of the icon filename containing non-ASCII characters.

**Actual behavior:**
- When icon filename contains non-ASCII characters: The project shows default Godot icon
- When icon filename uses only ASCII characters: The icon updates correctly
- This behavior is consistent for both PNG and SVG formats

**Additional context:**
- The issue is related to filename character encoding, not the file format

- This appears to be a filename handling issue rather than a format support issue

summary: GodotPhysics: Massive physics lag with complex concave collider triggered when `NaN` force is applied

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4-stable

### System information

Godot v4.4.stable - Ubuntu 24.04.2 LTS 24.04 on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 (nvidia; 550.120) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4770 CPU @ 3.40GHz (8 threads)

### Issue description

Physics server starts lagging massively when :
1. the scene contains a complex concave collider
2. the scene also contains another physic object with any collider type
3. and a `NaN` force is applied somewhere

The more complex the concave collider, the stronger the lag.
This only affects Godot Physics (not JOLT).
This only happens when a second collider (no matter its type or complexity) belongs to the scene.

I could pause during the lag and get a stack trace.

This shows that a `WorkerThreadPool` thread is recursively going through the whole collision shape's binary tree while the main thread is waiting for completion.
In another project with much more complex concave colliders, I'm getting all the `WorkerThreadPool` threads simultaneously running those massively recursive calls, resulting in a complete freeze of the physics server.

```gdscript
extends RigidBody3D

var trigger = false

func _input(event: InputEvent) -> void:
	if event is InputEventKey && (event as InputEventKey).keycode == KEY_SPACE :
		trigger = true
		
func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
	$Time.text = str(Time.get_ticks_msec())
	if trigger : apply_central_force(Vector3() / Vector3()) # NaN Vector3
```

summary: Audio popping when inside an Area3D with a reverb bus enabled and an amplifier on the master bus

description.text:
### Tested versions

Discovered on 4.4.stable
Reproducible in 4.4.dev4
Not reproducible on 4.4.dev3 or earlier

### System information

Godot v4.4.dev4 - Windows 10.0.19045 - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.6636) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-8700K CPU @ 3.70GHz (12 threads)

### Issue description

When I have an AudioStreamPlayer3D inside an Area3D with reverb bus enabled, AND that reverb bus goes to the Master bus which as an amplify effect set to a low negative value (-22 for example), the audio player pops at the beginning. It ONLY happens when the audio is routed through the reverb bus via an Area3D. If I manually set the audio bus on the AudioStreamPlayer3D this does not reproduce the issue. This is troublesome for my game which uses this footstep sound (it happens for other sounds too), and I'm not quite sure why it's happening. Any help would be appreciated.

Video of issue:
https://drive.google.com/file/d/15mTZVlw0Hp77dBJlKRprKh3nXIx1bFlr/view?usp=sharing
I'm not sure why I couldn't directly upload the video

### Steps to reproduce

Create an AudioStreamPlayer3D and put a stream inside it
Create and Area3D with reverb bus enabled, set to a bus with reverb, and with an amount of 1
Add an amplify effect to the master audio bus and set it to a low value like -25
Create a Camera3D set to current at the same position as the audio player
Create a script to play the sound when enter is pressed
Run the project

summary: Editor window does not regain mouse-in-window state when tooltip popup closes via mouse entry [macOS Display Server]

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible on master as of #103788.
Not reproducible on 4.4 (#100682 shadowed this issue making it not apparent).

### System information

macOS 15.3

### Issue description

Certain interface elements cause a tooltip popup to appear when hovered with the mouse. When the mouse exits the element, the popup disappears.

However, in the case where the mouse exits the element because it entered the tooltip's popup window, the main editor window does not return to a mouse-entered status when the popup disappears.

In this state, the interface does not behave as expected and certain elements cannot be interacted with via mouse.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open editor
2. Hover the mouse over an element that creates a (non-specialized) tooltip, eg the Output panel button
3. Move the mouse directly onto the tooltip popup

Observe that the editor now believes it is not focused: mousing over interface elements no longer highlights them, and elements such as buttons cannot be clicked.

(In debug builds, a warning is also printed stating: `Entering a window while a window is hovered should never happen in DisplayServer`).

This problematic state can be restored to normal by exiting the mouse and re-entering it into the editor window.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

summary: Changing exported type will not clear or warn about old assigned value type

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5-[fc827bb](https://github.com/godotengine/godot/commit/fc827bbe256e47e1d7ec6945deb9c1e79724dac9), 4.4-stable, 4.3-stable
- Not reproducible in: 3.6

### System information

Windows 11 / Linux

### Issue description

When exporting a property and giving it a value via the editor, then changing the type of the property, the value will persist.
They should instead at least warn the user. In 3.6 it does with a `Failed to set a resource of the type 'Curve2D' because this EditorResourcePicker only accepts 'Curve3D' and its derivatives.` message and (half) clears the value. Now it does neither. A quick look at the source code reveals that the message should come from editor_resource_picker.cpp:945 but I could not see any calls to the associated function in 4.5-fc827bb. Only in 3.6 (obviously)

### Steps to reproduce

1. Export a property in script (e.g. Curve2D or for assigned values: Node2D)
2. Set a value in editor
3. Change type of property to an unrelated type in script (e.g Curve3D or for assigned values: Node3D)
4. Value still persists
5. Running the project will set the value in game to null

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[TypeTest.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19324233/TypeTest.zip)
Exported properties should have wrong value assigned.

summary: Unable to reset an exported field to default when multiple nodes are selected.

description.text:
### Tested versions

4.4

### System information

Windows 11 - i5 13600kf - rtx 4070

### Issue description

When you select a node with exported field and change this field it's possible to reset it to default with a circle arrow icon but if you select multiple nodes (same type) the circle arrow is gone and you can't reset them to default which leads to you having to manually reset the nodes fields one by one which with even just a couple of nodes is annoying. Imagine you change values on twenty nodes and you then want to reset them, you have to click on every node individually to click the reset button.

This does not happen with build in fields as far as I know (for example do you want to reset Transform of 500 nodes? No problem, just select them and once click on the reset to default icon and it's fine).

Video: https://youtu.be/SiEi30JGtRU

### Steps to reproduce

1. Add a node with a script `@export var tt : float = 3` couple times to the world.
2. Change the tt field on all those nodes.
3. Select one and click on the icon to reset it to default.
4. Now Click on all the other ones and try to reset to default.
5. Observe the reset icon missing when multiple nodes are selected.

I'll add it later when I get back.

2. Run the main scene.

4. Note how the other sphere isn't (while behaving a bit odd, which is expected).

summary: Android: Convert `compress_native_libraries` to a basic export option

description.text: This PR moves `compress_native_libraries` to the basic export options, making it more visible to users. This change helps prevent confusion, as seen in issue https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/104137, where a user switched to the Gradle build and saw their apk size _more than double_. While the size increase will still occur, this change ensures that more users are aware of the option to compress native libraries, rather than it being hidden behind the `Advanced Options` toggle.

summary: CharacterBody2D get_position_delta returns initial transform at first run

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in Godot Engine v4.4.stable.official [4c311cbee]. ### System information Godot v4.4.stable - macOS Sequoia (15.3.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M1 Max - Apple M1 Max (10 threads) ### Issue description CharacterBody2D.get_position_delta() is documented so that it should return the travel (position delta) that occurred **during the last call** to move_and_slide(). However, if it is called before move_and_slide(), it returns the distance, that was defined by initial Transform -> Position property of the CharacterBody2D. The CharacterBody2D.get_position_delta() should return probably Vector2.ZERO if move_and_slide() has not been called yet. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Create simple 2D project, create 2D scene. 2. Insert nodes for CharacterBody2D (and a StaticBody2D for instance) alongside with their collision shapes. 3. Transform the CharacterBody2D so its initial position.y is significantly higher than 0.0 (i. e. 1000). 4. Add a default basic movement script to CharacterBody2D. 5. In _physics(delta: float) function measure the distance fallen by calling get_position_delta(), print it to the console. 6. The first get_position_delta() call (even before move_and_slide() is called) returns initial transform coordinates. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [get_position_delta_Minimal_Project.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/19309332/get_position_delta_Minimal_Project.zip)

summary: queue_free() and free() sometimes crash game without errors or warnings

description.text:
### Tested versions

Godot v4.4.stable

### System information

macOS Sequoia (15.3.1) - Single-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M3 - Apple M3 (8 threads)

### Issue description

calling queue_free(), free() or call_deferred(“free”) sometimes causes the game to crash when changing scenes.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new project.
2. Make a scene with a Control (or other node) as the root node.
3. Add a button to the scene.
4. Attach a script to the Control (or other node).
5. Connect the button’s pressed signal to the Control (or other node).
6. Make a new scene and save it.
7. In the previous scene, add to the _on_button_pressed() function this:
    get_tree().change_scene_to_file("res://other_scene.tscn")
    $”.".queue_free() # Remember to edit the paths so that they are accurate.
8. Run the scene a few times. Sometimes the window will crash after pressing the button, sometimes it will not.
9. Try removing queue_free() from the script and running it again. It should work just fine.

I'm using GridMap in my 3D isometric turn-based game for some part of the gameplay, like for example the fights. ### Steps to reproduce

- Open the project
- Run main scene
- Move mouse around white cells, they are supposed to become red using SetCellType
- You should see weird flickering

Then: 

- Disable 3D physics interpolation
- Redo steps above
- No flickering
